key_insight: WeldMamba predicts the future weld-pool layout from past images and process signals, enabling lookahead segmentation of keyhole, wire, and molten-pool regions for delayed closed-loop control.
engineering_relevance: This is directly relevant to real-time laser wire-feed welding because it helps compensate for sensing and actuation delay, improving control readiness before the physical weld state changes.
method: Uses historical coaxial grayscale images plus welding parameters and wire-state electrical signals as inputs.
Combines a visual encoder with process/sensor-conditioned normalization and patch-level temporal state space modeling.
Adds horizon-conditioned latent prediction, dense future feature prediction, and a motion-aware mask decoder with physics-guided losses.
evidence_signal: On a 43-sequence laser welding dataset, the method achieved 74.63% mIoU at a 500 ms lookahead, with ablations showing temporal history, patch-level state space modeling, and keyhole motion awareness were most important.
watch_item: Performance depends on well-aligned multimodal signals and may be sensitive to domain shift or sensor timing errors in new welding setups.
paper_title: Physics-Guided Spatiotemporal State Space Modeling for Lookahead Molten Pool Segmentation in Laser Wire-Feed Welding
